text: The surgeon performs a robotic-assisted hand-sewn anastomosis on the distal segment of the colon, which is visibly too long for an EA stapler.
The surgeon creates the posterior layer of the anastomosis using a running IIO silk suture, incorporating the staple line to both oversew it and form the posterior serosal layer.
The surgeon creates enterotomies on both the distal and proximal segments of the colon, each spanning the width of the staple line to facilitate a wide anastomosis.

The surgeon closes the mucosal layer of the anastomosis in two directions using two separate 3-0 Vicryl sutures. The third robotic arm is utilized to maintain tension on the suture line, ensuring clear visualization of the surgical area.

The surgeon uses a vicryl suture to close the anterior portion of the anastomosis, then begins a second suture on the posterior layer, proceeding in the opposite direction. The mucosal layer is completed as the two sutures meet in the middle and are tied together.
The surgeon closes the front layer of the anastomosis using a separate running silk suture, tying it at the end. All suture material is then removed, completing the anastomosis.
The completed anastomosis is observed lying tension-free within the abdominal cavity.
